title: salvia aurita var. galpinii
intro: Salvia aurita var. galpinii, also known as the 'twisted white sage' or 'crested sage', is a variant of the Salvia aurita species, a flowering plant native to South Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. Salvia aurita var. galpinii has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant properties, which help ease coughs and congestion. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
